title: Social Security for a Surviving Spouse: What Changes
summary: A surviving spouse's own Social Security situation often changes in ways worth understanding early.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Social Security for a Surviving Spouse: What Changes | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: A surviving spouse's own Social Security situation often changes in ways worth understanding early.
whatYouNeedToKnow: A surviving spouse may be able to switch to a higher survivor benefit.
Remarriage rules can affect eligibility, depending on age.
A one-time death benefit may also be available.

paragraphs: A surviving spouse already receiving their own Social Security retirement benefit may be eligible to switch to a survivor benefit if it's higher, rather than simply continuing on their own smaller benefit — this isn't automatic and typically needs to be requested.
There's also a modest one-time lump-sum death benefit available to some surviving spouses, worth asking about directly since it isn't always mentioned proactively.

nextSteps: Ask Social Security directly whether switching to a survivor benefit makes sense.
Ask about the one-time death benefit.
Clarify how remarriage, if relevant, affects eligibility.
disclaimer: General information — Social Security can calculate the specific benefit comparison for your situation.
